inputs.name: image
inputs.semantic_hint: Preprocessed RGB image fed as a single-element list into the YOLOv7 model forward call.

ground_truths.name: bounding_boxes
ground_truths.semantic_hint: Per-image ground-truth bounding box tensor encoding spatial coordinates (x, y, w, h) and class label, padded to MAX_BB_PER_IMAGE entries.

proposed_mapping.name: get_bb
proposed_mapping.notes: signature=get_bb(idx: int, subset) -> np.ndarray; output_shape=[150, 5]; coordinate_format=xywh

unknowns: leap_binder.py source file is absent from repository; only compiled .pyc exists at __pycache__/leap_binder.cpython-39.pyc. Functions input_image, get_bb, subset_images_list, bbs_metadata, image_path, origin_path, image_metadata, gt_decoder, bb_decoder, od_loss (re-export), regression_metric, classification_metric, object_metric are all defined there. Their exact preprocessing logic (normalization, padding strategy, coordinate conversion) cannot be verified without source.

Raw bounding box coordinate format in annotation file is ambiguous: preprocess.py parses as integer tuples but does not clarify if they are (x1,y1,x2,y2) xyxy or (x,y,w,h) xywh. The xywh inference comes from downstream metrics.py usage of xywh_to_xyxy_format on gt_loc extracted from y_true.

subset_images_list() (from leap_binder) controls which dataset splits are used. Without source, the exact split strategy (train/valid as per config TRAINING_CATEGORIES) and whether an unlabeled split is returned cannot be confirmed.
